summary: EC8 Phase 8a (Employees & Team Foundation) fully tested end-to-end — backend (30/30 pytest: 16 pre-existing + 14 new spot-check tests) and frontend (all UI flows via Playwright). No bugs found. All 15 requested feature/bug checks passed: Team Dashboard status counts + announcements panel, Employees list/create/search, Employee Detail (edit fields, conditional Save button, status change dialog excluding current status, status history, Activity tab audit timeline), Announcements draft->publish, sidebar flyout enablement/disablement (Overview/Employees/Announcements enabled; Tasks & Kanban/Team Schedule/Time Clock/Timesheets/Payroll/Messages & Notes/Employee Portal correctly disabled with 'SOON' badges and non-clickable), flyout z-index fix confirmed (fully opaque, no bleed-through), 401 unauthenticated + owner full-access + tenant isolation (via existing test_ec8_employees.py staff-role 403 tests), and EC0-EC7 regression spot-check (Home, Customers, Orders, Inventory all load cleanly, no console errors).

issues: Uses native browser date input (type="date", renders as mm/dd/yyyy picker) instead of the shadcn Calendar/DatePicker component used elsewhere in the app. Cosmetic inconsistency, not a functional bug.

action_items: (Optional polish, not blocking) Replace native date input on EmployeeDetailPage Hire date field with the shadcn Calendar component for visual consistency with the rest of the app.
critical_code_review_comments: Permission enum rename in core/permissions.py (EMPLOYEE_READ/EMPLOYEE_MANAGE etc.) is clean — old superseded values fully removed, verified by test_superseded_ec1_permission_values_removed passing and no route referencing old names.
employee_service.py / announcement_service.py correctly follow router->service->direct DB access pattern (no repository classes), consistent with owner-approved decision.
Tenant isolation correctly enforced via {tenant_id} filter on every DB query in both services; cross-tenant employee lookup returns 404 (verified via test_employee_tenant_isolation and UI reasoning).

Employee status change correctly rejects same-status transitions server-side (400) and the frontend proactively excludes the current status from the dropdown, preventing that error from ever being user-triggerable — good defensive UX matching the request's expectation.
Announcement publish delivery only reaches employees with linked_user_id — documented as an intentional Phase 8a limitation, not a bug.

Sidebar flyout entries for Phase 8b+ (Time Clock, Timesheets, Payroll, Team Schedule, Tasks & Kanban, Messages & Notes, Employee Portal) are correctly disabled with 'SOON' badges, cursor-not-allowed styling, and do not navigate on click — scope boundary respected as instructed.
AppShell z-index fix holds: flyout panel renders fully opaque over page content (verified via screenshot), and clicking an enabled entry closes the flyout and navigates correctly.
test_credentials: AUTH_DEV_BYPASS=true — dev-login auto-authenticates as 'Dev Owner' (role=owner, tenant='Dev Shop'). No manual credentials needed. /app/memory/test_credentials.md is empty (no auth setup required for this checkpoint).
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_-prefixed employees (e.g. TEST_UI_Employee_Playwright, several via pytest spot-check) and TEST_-prefixed announcements (TEST_Announcement_..., TEST_UI_Announcement_Playwright) in the Dev Shop tenant via both API tests and UI Playwright flows. Not cleaned up — low risk since dev tenant is disposable and all test data is clearly TEST_-prefixed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: EC8 Phase 8a is fully verified — Employees, Announcements, Team Dashboard, permission enforcement, tenant isolation, and nav scope boundary all work correctly. Dev Shop tenant now has several TEST_-prefixed employees (statuses: active, suspended) and announcements (draft/published) left over from this testing session — safe to reuse or ignore in next iteration. Full EC0-EC7 regression was intentionally NOT run per owner's credit-conservation instruction; only Home/Customers/Orders/Inventory were spot-checked and loaded cleanly. When Phase 8b (Time Clock) is authorized, verify flyout-time-clock etc. become enabled and routes get built.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found — this was a clean, well-scoped delivery. No RCA needed.
